input: Create a NightSkyIllustration for a sleep/evening mode — full moon, Milky Way arc, silhouetted hills
Add a PantryIllustration showing shelves of preserved jars, root vegetables, and hanging dried herbs
failures: COMPLETE_SCENE: The output is truncated mid-render — it cuts off partway through Jar 5 on Shelf 1, leaving Shelf 2, Shelf 3, hanging dried herbs, and root vegetables either incomplete or entirely missing. The scene is not fully drawn.

input: Create a SeedlingIllustration for a spring-start widget — small pots in a tray, just sprouting
failures: PAPER_CUTOUT_STYLE: The illustration lacks the layered paper cut-out aesthetic. The pots are rendered as circles (which are not flat geometric cut-out shapes typical of the style), and there are no distinct stacked depth planes beyond a simple sky/ground split. The sprouts use stroke-only paths rather than filled flat shapes. A true paper cut-out style requires bold filled silhouette shapes stacked at multiple visible depth planes (e.g., background hills, mid-ground shelf/tray as a distinct plane, foreground pots as solid trapezoid/polygon shapes). Only 2 clear color-layer planes are present (sky and ground), falling short of the required 4 visible shape layers.

input: Update the GardenIllustration to add a trellis with climbing vines on the right side
failures: PAPER_CUTOUT_STYLE: The illustration uses a linearGradient on the sky background, which violates the flat paper cut-out aesthetic. All layers should be flat solid colors, not gradients. Additionally, the trellis is built from SVG <line> elements rather than flat filled path/polygon shapes, which doesn't match the paper cut-out layered approach.

COLOR_VARIETY: The palette is dominated by greens and earth tones (#87CEEB sky is acceptable but the gradient undermines it). There are no warm or light accent colors (amber, rust, cream, yellow, warm highlights) used for the vines, flowers, or trellis. The trellis brown (#8B6F47) is the only non-green element, but the flower clusters (which would add warm color contrast) are cut off — the output is incomplete/truncated before the flower elements are rendered.

input: Create a SunriseIllustration for the morning home screen — fog lifting over hills, amber sky, rooster silhouette
failures: PAPER_CUTOUT_STYLE: The illustration relies heavily on gradients (sunriseGradient, sunGradient) and blur filters (fogMist) rather than flat colored shapes stacked at distinct depth planes. The paper cut-out aesthetic requires solid flat colors at each layer — not gradient fills or feGaussianBlur fog effects. The layering structure (far hill, mid hill, ground) is present but the rendering technique is wrong.

COLOR_VARIETY: The sky uses a dark purple-to-amber gradient rather than distinct flat palette values. The hills are all very similar dark brown tones (#4A3728, #6B5344, #7C6B52) with no lighter or warmer accent colors in the foreground. The rooster is entirely #1A1A1A. There are no lighter/cream/warm amber flat shapes used as accents to provide contrast — the scene reads as uniformly dark and muddy without meaningful color range across depth planes.
